# Risk severity

When Vault Radar finds a risk, it attempts to assign a severity to the risk to
help users prioritize which risks to remediate first.

```mermaid
---
title: "HCP Vault Radar Severity Flowchart"
---
flowchart LR
    A@{ shape: rounded, label: "Vault Radar Finding" } --> B{Is the finding a Secret?}
    B --> |Yes| E{Is there a configured Event Rule for the secret type? - online scans only}
    E --> |No| F{Is the Secret tagged with any of the following: google_maps_api_key, google_calendar_api_key, test_secret_type, experimental, inactive_secret}
    F --> |No| H{Is the Secret Active?}
    H --> |Yes| J{Is the Secret in the latest version of the content?}
    J ---> |Yes| CRITICAL@{ shape: stadium, label: "Critical!" }
    J ---> |No| HIGH@{ shape: stadium, label: "High" }
    H --> |No| K{Is the Secret in the latest version of a secret manager?}
    K --> |Yes| HIGH
    K --> |No| I{Is the finding tagged with any of the following: secret_in_test_file, secret_in_example_file, example_secret, secret_in_dev_file, secret_in_readme_file, secret_in_staging_file, simple_password, may_be_encrypted_password, common_word_in_secret, test_secret, third_party_secret, secret_in_translation_file, low_entropy}
    I --> |No| MEDIUM@{ shape: stadium, label: "Medium - Default" }
    B --> |No| C{Is the finding PII or NIL?}
    C --> |PII| LOW@{ shape: stadium, label: "Low" }
    C ------> |NIL| INFO@{ shape: stadium, label: "Informational" }
    F --> |Yes| INFO
    I --> |Yes| LOW
    E ------> |YES| EVENT-RULE@{ shape: stadium, label: "Set the severity to the configured value:<br> Informational, Low, Medium, High, or Critical" }
```

## Risk severity definitions

### Critical

This means the risk category is a some type of secret. The secret is active and
the secret is in the latest version of content within a resource or
a secret manager.

### High

This means the risk category is a some type of secret. The secret is active but
the secret is NOT in the latest version of content within a resource.

It can also mean the risk category is a some type of secret. The activeness
is unknown but it is in the latest version of a secret manager.

### Medium

If the item meets no other conditions, Vault Radar sets the severity to medium by
default.

### Low

Vault Radar sets risks to low if it meets one of the following conditions:

- The risk category is Personally Identifiable Information (PII)
- The risk has certain tags (for example, TagSecretInTestFile, TagSecretInExampleFile)

### Info

Vault Radar sets risks to info if it meets one of the following conditions:

- Risk category is non-inclusive language (NIL)
- The risk has certain tags (for example, TagIgnoreRule, TagGoogleMapsApiKey, TagInactiveSecret).
- The risk category is AwsAccessKeyId and the risk is not active.
